ranked_movies.title: Parasite
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently mentioned as a masterpiece and a key film in the debate between Bong Joon-ho and Park Chan-wook. Described as 'top notch' and a benchmark for modern Korean cinema.

ranked_movies.title: Oldboy
ranked_movies.reason: Cited as one of the best films ever seen and a major reason for choosing Park Chan-wook's filmography. A cornerstone of the vengeance trilogy.

ranked_movies.title: The Handmaiden
ranked_movies.reason: Highly recommended and considered by some to be superior to 'Parasite'. Praised for its aesthetics, versatility, and being a prime example of Park Chan-wook's work.

ranked_movies.title: Memories of Murder
ranked_movies.reason: Described as the best film from both directors' filmographies. Its existence was a pivotal point in the debate, with some users stating it made choosing difficult.

ranked_movies.title: Decision to Leave
ranked_movies.reason: Ranked alongside 'The Handmaiden' as a reason to choose Park Chan-wook. Mentioned for its aesthetic beauty and tactile filmmaking style.

ranked_movies.title: Save the Green Planet!
ranked_movies.reason: The original South Korean film that 'Bugonia' is a remake of. Its mention in the box office forecast brings attention to this cult classic.

ranked_movies.title: I Saw the Devil
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended by a user as an 'awesome movie' from another great Korean director, Kim Jee-woon, showing its high regard within the genre.

ranked_movies.title: Mother
ranked_movies.reason: Grouped with 'Parasite' and 'Memories of Murder' as part of Bong Joon-ho's 'top notch' filmography.

ranked_movies.title: Train to Busan
ranked_movies.reason: Directly referenced in a 'Star Trek' discussion, confirming its status as a well-known modern classic, especially in the zombie genre.

ranked_movies.title: The Good, The Bad, The Weird
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended alongside 'I Saw the Devil' as another great film from director Kim Jee-woon, noted for its entertainment value.

ranked_movies.title: Sympathy for Mr. Vengeance
ranked_movies.reason: Implied as part of Park Chan-wook's highly consistent and acclaimed filmography, which includes multiple masterpieces.

ranked_movies.title: Lady Vengeance
ranked_movies.reason: Implied as part of Park Chan-wook's highly consistent and acclaimed filmography, completing the renowned vengeance trilogy.

ranked_movies.title: Snowpiercer
ranked_movies.reason: While not explicitly named, it is a major part of Bong Joon-ho's filmography and would be included in discussions about his work.

ranked_movies.title: The Host
ranked_movies.reason: A significant film in Bong Joon-ho's career, it would naturally be part of any discussion comparing his work to Park Chan-wook's.

ranked_movies.title: Joint Security Area
ranked_movies.reason: An earlier, highly regarded work from Park Chan-wook, contributing to his reputation for consistency and quality.

ranked_movies.title: Thirst
ranked_movies.reason: Another film from Park Chan-wook's diverse filmography, showcasing his ability to switch genres with ease, from thrillers to romance/horror.

ranked_movies.title: Barking Dogs Never Bite
ranked_movies.reason: Bong Joon-ho's debut feature, representing his more experimental early work that was mentioned in the discussions.

ranked_movies.title: Okja
ranked_movies.reason: Part of Bong Joon-ho's filmography that, while not mentioned directly, is a notable Netflix film from the director.

ranked_movies.title: Dawn of the Dead
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned in the 'Star Trek' thread alongside 'Train to Busan' due to the actor Ken Foree, cementing its classic status in the zombie genre.

ranked_movies.title: A Tale of Two Sisters
ranked_movies.reason: A iconic Korean horror film from director Kim Jee-woon, its reputation makes it a likely candidate for recommendation within the broader context of Korean cinema discussions.
